speaker: Grandfather
text: Come in, come in! Your grandmother and I have been waiting all morning. Let me look at you — you've grown a foot since Christmas!

speaker: Pre-teen Boy
text: Hi Grandpa! I missed you. Grandma said you have a surprise for us.

speaker: Grandmother
text: I might have said something like that. Come to the kitchen, I just took a pie out of the oven.

speaker: Small Girl
text: Is it apple pie? Please say it's apple pie!

speaker: Grandmother
text: What other kind of pie would I make? Of course it's apple, sweetheart.

speaker: Grandfather
text: But before the pie, I want to show you something in the garage. I found my old train set in a box and I thought you two might want to set it up.

speaker: Pre-teen Boy
text: A real train set? Like with tracks and a locomotive?

speaker: Grandfather
text: The very one your father used to play with when he was your age. Still works beautifully.

speaker: Small Girl
text: Can I drive the train, Grandpa?

speaker: Grandfather
text: You can be the conductor. Most important job on the whole railway.

speaker: Grandmother
text: I'll have the pie and milk ready by the time you're done playing. Don't be too long, it's best warm.

speaker: Pre-teen Boy
text: This is the best Saturday ever.

speaker: Grandfather
text: Ha. Every Saturday you come to visit is the best Saturday, if you ask me.
